summary: Large fighter-collision decomp PR focused on src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c. It replaced many NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs with typed fighter/item collision, damage-log, knockback, hurtbox-init, shield/absorb/reflect, stale-move, and effect-spawn logic, then updated ftcoll.h and ft/types.h to support the new code. decomp.dev reported +1688 matched-code bytes with 8 new full matches and 13 improved unmatched ftcoll items, but also reported a small .sdata match regression.

key_files.role: Main decomp work; 1816 additions / 101 deletions across 27 hunks. Implemented or improved ftColl collision/damage/effect routines and added matching scaffolding.
Replaced several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or loose prototypes with typed signatures for ftColl_80077970, ftColl_80077C60, ftColl_80078538, ftColl_80078998, and ftColl_80079C70.
Typed ftData.x30 as a count/inits hurtbox-init structure, ftDynamics.x8 as struct ftData_x38*, Fighter.x1670[0].x24 as int, and DmgLogEntry.x20 as float.
Added EntityKind_UNKNOWN to enum EntityKind for ftcoll damage-log switch/default handling.

what_changed: Implemented multiple previously stubbed ftcoll routines, including ftColl_80077688, ftColl_80077970, ftColl_80077C60, ftColl_80078538, ftColl_800787B4, ftColl_8007891C, ftColl_80078998, ftColl_80079AB0, ftColl_80079C70, ftColl_80079EA8, ftColl_8007A06C, ftColl_8007ABD0, ftColl_8007AC9C, ftColl_8007B320, ftColl_8007B4E0, ftColl_8007BAC0, and ftColl_8007BC90.

Added item-versus-fighter collision handling in ftColl_8007925C, covering item hitboxes against fighter hit capsules/hurt capsules, team/owner filtering, reflect, absorb, shield, catch-like item interactions, and SFX/effect side effects.

Added item shield and hitbox interaction routines: ftColl_80077688 records shield damage, outgoing damage direction, owner/player attribution, and shield-hit effects; ftColl_80077970 handles hitbox clank-style item/fighter interactions; ftColl_80077C60 handles item damage application, tip logs, special item pickup/counter cases, and stale-move updates through ftColl_80078998.

Implemented damage-result processing in ftColl_8007A06C, selecting the highest knockback damage-log entry, computing direction/angle/element/source/damage output, spawning appropriate effects, and dispatching ftColl_8007861C for fighter, item, or environment damage sources.

Added knockback/damage helper formulas in ftColl_80079AB0, ftColl_80079C70, and ftColl_80079EA8 using fighter weight, Player_GetAttackRatio, Player_GetDefenseRatio, gm_8016B248 stage scaling, hit x24/x28/x2C data, percent/temp damage, and p_ftCommonData caps.
Updated ftColl_80076ED8 matching/control flow and corrected one damage-log write from dmg_log1/dmg_log1_idx to dmg_log0/dmg_log0_idx in the damage-log0 path.
Converted several OSReport + HSD_ASSERT or __assert pairs into HSD_ASSERTREPORT with explicit line constants such as 0xB7, 0xE3, 0xF9, 0x110, 0x888, 0x8C9, and 0x8DF.

Added required includes for newly referenced fighter, item, player, collision, and math APIs, including ft_0881.h, ft_0DF0.h, ftchangeparam.h, it_2725.h, pl_040D.h, player.h, pltrick.h, and MSL/trigf.h.
smart_moves: Typed public ftcoll prototypes as soon as implementations revealed real parameters, reducing UNK usage and making later call-site work safer.

Used struct discoveries from the implementations to improve headers: ftData_x30 exposes hurtbox-init count/inits, ftDynamics.x8 is no longer void*, and DmgLogEntry.x20 is correctly treated as float for fractional/tip damage.
Preserved matching-sensitive control flow with PAD_STACK, #pragma dont_inline, explicit goto labels such as retfalse/catch_path/catch_elem_path, and duplicated inline math where needed.
Kept damage source handling explicit by tagging DmgLogEntry.x0 and branching for fighter, item, and environment-style entries in ftColl_8007A06C.
Fixed a high-risk log-array mismatch in ftColl_80076ED8 by writing damage-log0 entries to dmg_log0[dmg_log0_idx] instead of dmg_log1[dmg_log1_idx].
Centralized report/assert behavior into HSD_ASSERTREPORT calls, which better matches emitted code than separate OSReport and assert calls in several ftcoll paths.
decomp_lessons: Collision decomp often forces header progress: once a routine walks data tables, replace opaque pointers with minimal structs that expose only proven fields, as done for ftData_x30 and ftDynamics.x8.
Damage logs should preserve float damage until the assembly clearly truncates; this PR changed DmgLogEntry.x20 from int to float and only casts at specific call sites.

For ftcoll matching, source entity kind matters: fighter, item, and environment-like damage entries share final result output but require different attack ratios, direction logic, owner attribution, and effect dispatch.
Small type choices can affect matching; dmg_log0_idx and dmg_log1_idx were changed from size_t to int even though the arrays are indexed by bounded counts.
Item collision code is cross-system code. Future work should expect dependencies on item owner, teams, stale moves, player stats, effects, shield state, absorb/reflect state, and game-mode checks.
Generated decomp.dev reports are useful for balancing progress against regressions: this PR gained many function matches but left a .sdata break that future work should investigate.
naming_conventions: Address-based ftColl_* function names were retained, but prototypes now use domain types such as Item*, HitCapsule*, Fighter*, Fighter_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, and Vec3* instead of UNK_PARAMS.
The new enum value is named EntityKind_UNKNOWN, using an all-caps UNKNOWN suffix alongside EntityKind_None, EntityKind_Fighter, and EntityKind_Item.
New struct naming follows existing placeholder style: struct ftData_x30 contains count and inits, while ftDynamics.x8 is typed as struct ftData_x38*.
Damage-log globals keep the existing dmg_log0/dmg_log1 and dmg_log0_idx/dmg_log1_idx naming rather than introducing semantic names.
Evidence-backed field names such as xC34_damageDealt, xCB8_outDamageDirection, x19A0_shieldDamageTaken, and xCF4_fighterGObjUnk are reused rather than renamed speculatively.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK with several exact sizes, including 8, 16, 20, 24, 32, 46, and 0xB0, to shape stack frames for matching.
Used #pragma dont_inline around matching-sensitive functions such as ftColl_8007891C, ftColl_80078998, ftColl_8007925C, ftColl_8007BC90, and ftColl_8007BE3C.
Used labels and gotos to reproduce original control flow, including retfalse in ftColl_80076ED8 and skip_hit_loop/catch_path/catch_elem_path in ftColl_8007925C.
Relied on named float constants like ftColl_804D82E0, ftColl_804D82E8, ftColl_804D82EC, ftColl_804D82F0, ftColl_804D8314, ftColl_804D8318, and ftColl_804D831C instead of simplifying to literals.
Kept explicit casts among float, int, u32, and size_t in damage and knockback formulas, especially around hit->damage, hit->unk_count, hit->x24, hit->x28, and DmgLogEntry.size_of_xC.
Used MSL trig functions cosf, sinf, and atanf in collision/effect angle paths rather than replacing them with approximate helpers.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code for GALE01 increased to 62.55% (+0.04%, +1688 bytes); matched data was 38.10% (-8 bytes).
Reported 8 new full matches in main/melee/ft/ftcoll: ftColl_8007BC90, ftColl_800787B4, ftColl_80078538, ftColl_8007ABD0, ftColl_80078998, ftColl_8007891C, ftColl_8007AC9C, and ftColl_8007BBCC.

Reported 13 improvements in unmatched ftcoll items, including ftColl_8007A06C to 87.21%, ftColl_8007925C to 97.54%, ftColl_80077C60 to 97.11%, ftColl_80077970 to 96.62%, and ftColl_80077688 to 92.44%.
Reported one broken match: main/melee/ft/ftcoll .sdata dropped from 100.00% to 28.57% (-6 bytes).
No human reviews or review comments were captured for this PR, so reviewer intent beyond the bot report is not evidenced.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fix the ftcoll .sdata regression reported after PR 2398.
Search for later edits to DmgLogEntry.x20, dmg_log0_idx, or dmg_log1_idx to confirm the float/int typing held.
Search for ftData_x30 and ftDynamics.x8 usage in later fighter-data/hurtbox decomp PRs.
Search for future work on ftColl_8007925C because PR 2398 brought it to 97.54% but not a full match.
Search for HSD_ASSERTREPORT conversions in ftcoll.c to reuse exact report/assert matching patterns.
Search for later renames or documentation of EntityKind_UNKNOWN and DmgLogEntry.x0 values.
